# Source Card: Comparison of estimated GFR using cystatin C versus creatinine in pediatric kidney transplant recipients

## Summary
According to PubMed, this single-center comparative study ([DOI](https://doi.org/10.1007/s00467-024-06316-6), PMID 38427073) evaluated seven creatinine-, cystatin-C-, and combination-based eGFR equations against iohexol-measured GFR in 45 pediatric kidney transplant recipients aged 1-18 years. For the pediatric CDS module it establishes equation-specific performance metrics (bias, precision, P30/P10 accuracy, CKD-stage misclassification) rather than diagnostic reference intervals. The authors conclude cystatin-C-based equations (with or without creatinine) may have modestly better bias, precision, and accuracy than creatinine-only equations, while explicitly cautioning the difference may not be clinically significant.

## Key evidence (with exact-passage locators)
- (ev_001) Median iohexol mGFR 93.3 mL/min/1.73 m2 (n=45, age 1-18); banded 57.8% >=90 / 33.3% 60-89 / 8.9% 30-59 — locator: Results para 2 — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:998b8106c2b2be55f6df3c0badd3011f8883b99bcbd0e29a41e24707c5bcd250]"
- (ev_002) U25-CysC highest accuracy: P30 88.9%, P10 37.8% — locator: Results accuracy para — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:a43bd61f1dfc2b4f314a84b352b0b88a9eeba22d0e12b232fc7a5f119c45d839]"
- (ev_003) Gentian CysC smallest mean bias 0.1 mL/min/1.73 m2 (whole), -5.6 (histological subgroup) — locator: Results bias para — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:3bb1cff2f282b302d316f8110919027df167a3e44b40fbd73d2ef6d84b860790]"
- (ev_004) CAPA least CKD-stage misclassification: 24.4% whole / 33.3% histological; CKD G1 correct 64.4% — locator: Results CKD-staging para — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:b2a1e0fc8d8f7fc1fe035ae48aaf3c1102242ef1ef8f55d8f6a6d5c8fff2a1e2]"
- (ev_005) Overestimation region: Bedside Schwartz, Gentian CysC, CAPA, U25-CysC overestimate at GFR >100 — locator: Results Bland-Altman para — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:7ae0d42be35ed060e3f015e55a2b1cc9f8df4a442ba16db280e26af596c79858]"
- (ev_006) Bedside Schwartz equation eGFR = 0.413 x (ht[cm]/Cr[mg/dL]) — locator: Methods, Estimation of GFR eq.1 — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:faaa1222a69b7398b2de6c627df1ce33180f78ff81e4f2af5d6f77ff9fbcd9f4]"
- (ev_007) IFCC-standardized Gentian CysC; divide by 1.17 for pre-IFCC CKiD Cr-CysC equation — locator: Methods, Estimation of GFR — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:0def8d9334e9e9650530ad6c3bd6e051a0e7535ef224c824d13f7b53d350b37e]"

## Population / assay / lifecycle notes
- Population: pediatric kidney transplant recipients, age 1-18 y (median 12.5 y), 66.7% male, 62.2% deceased-donor, >=6 months post-transplant, stable graft function, 95.6% steroid-based immunosuppression (prednisone/prednisolone 0.07-0.1 mg/kg/day, max 5 mg/day).
- Assay/method dependence is material: Cr = enzymatic (Olympus); CysC = turbidimetric (Gentian AS, Moss, Norway), IFCC-standardized. Coefficients and thresholds are assay-calibration specific — the pre-IFCC CKiD Cr-CysC equation requires dividing Gentian CysC by 1.17. Steroids at low/medium dose reportedly do not alter CysC production; very high glucocorticoid doses do (none of the cohort was on high-dose steroids at study time).
- Lifecycle: published 2024-07 (Pediatr Nephrol 39(7):2177-2186); references CKiD U25, Bedside Schwartz (2009), CAPA, Gentian, CKiD Cr-CysC equations and K/DOQI CKD staging; no retirement/supersession asserted by the source.

## Scope-exit / referral relevance (if any)
- No explicit red-flag/referral threshold or scope boundary is defined. The clinical framing is that accurate eGFR monitoring enables "early detection of allograft dysfunction" and timely biopsy, but the paper prescribes no numeric trigger and issues no diagnosis/treatment directive. All metrics here are equation-performance descriptors, not executable CDS rules.

## Limitations & conflicts
- Equation-exponent GAP: the PMC full-text-to-markdown conversion dropped superscript exponents in several equations (e.g., Gentian CysC printed as "eGFR = 79.901 × CysC" and CAPA as "eGFR = 130 × CysC× age− 7" both lost their exponents; CKiD Cr-CysC exponents also lost). Therefore the FULL Gentian CysC, CAPA, U25 (K tables), and CKiD Cr-CysC equation forms are NOT locatable verbatim in this retrieval and are recorded as gaps — do NOT assert those coefficients/exponents from this card. Only Bedside Schwartz (ev_006, eGFR = 0.413 × ht/Cr, no exponent) and the /1.17 IFCC correction (ev_007) are cleanly locatable.
- Source typographic errors preserved, not corrected: units repeatedly print "1.73 m" / "1.72 m" where "1.73 m2" is intended (exponent-2 lost in conversion); ev_005 quote carries the source's "1.72 m" typo.
- Small single-center cohort (n=45), biopsy-triggered enrollment (selection bias), well-preserved graft function (median mGFR 93.3; 91.1% >60) — limits generalizability to advanced CKD and to <6-months-post-transplant.
- Conflicts preserved (not averaged): this study favors CysC-based equations, but de Souza et al. found CysC formulas NOT superior to Cr-based in pediatric transplant on low-dose steroids, and Selistre et al. / a 1139-patient adult cohort preferred Cr-based (alone or with CysC). The authors themselves state the difference "may not be clinically significant" and their data "does not demonstratively show superiority of one biomarker over the other."
- Thyroid function was not assessed (a known CysC confounder), acknowledged as a limitation by the authors.
